tweets.text: Join us as a Forward Deployed Engineer! https://t.co/pGwVx9ml8u

What we find most useful about CNA is that the intervention is simple yet powerful. The steering is a multiplicative ablation on a sparse set of MLP neurons, which makes CNA a clean addition on top of standard production pipelines: instruction tuning, RL, and safety post-training. We find the neuron basis to be ripe for further exploration in interpretability and steering domains.

Paper: https://t.co/NEVG9QCTDb
Blog: https://t.co/q8hknexg54
Code: https://t.co/BIxwoBg1tZ
HF: https://t.co/PzJKs0NA2t

CNA is a contribution from the mechanistic interpretability team at Nous. If you want to work on problems like this, find us on Discord.

x.name: Modal
x.bio:
AI infrastructure that developers love 💚

Run inference, sandboxes, batch processing, training, and many other things on Modal

tweets.text: Modal is proud to now support @claudeai Managed Agents with Modal Sandboxes. https://t.co/uHbChlSRdV https://t.co/Aj4wvbOuej

we've updated the claude-api skill to help onboard on self-hosted sandboxes. 

you can invoke this in the latest version of Claude Code (with the "/claude-api" command) and ask questions. 

see repo here:
https://t.co/wKlvKQDwJu

more cookbooks here:
https://t.co/WOYFBmkwHB

@vercel: use a Vercel Sandbox for fine control over both networking and credentials. Vercel runs compute inside / adjacent to your own network. lets you reach internal databases, private APIs, or services.

https://t.co/0vxiLJbYsv https://t.co/c2XcCXJ908

@modal: use custom images to set network security, scale to 100,000 concurrent sandboxes per customer. configure CPU, memory, & GPU resources.

https://t.co/3odQN9MNkh https://t.co/auSFck0eSR
